task_completion.description: Verify the Multiparameter Monitor device adapter is created and configured with the correct vital signs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an OpenICE device adapter window (e.g., 'Multiparameter Monitor') with input fields or displays showing Heart Rate set to 125, Respiratory Rate set to 28, and SpO2 set to 96.

task_completion.description: Verify the Infusion Pump device adapter is created and configured with the correct rate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an OpenICE device adapter window (e.g., 'Infusion Pump') with an input field or display showing the infusion rate set to 10.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Vital Signs' clinical application is open and displaying the configured data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenICE 'Vital Signs' clinical application window open and actively displaying the simulated patient data: HR 125, RR 28, and SpO2 96.

task_completion.description: Verify the screenshot of the Vital Signs application is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'pediatric_evidence.png' visible on the Desktop, in a file manager at /home/ga/Desktop/, or a screenshot tool actively saving to this exact path.

task_completion.description: Verify the configuration report is written to the correct path with the required information.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'pediatric_config.txt' on the Desktop, in a file manager at /home/ga/Desktop/, or an open text editor/terminal showing the file being written with the device names and configured values.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual OpenICE graphical interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the OpenICE application GUI windows (device adapters and Vital Signs app) are visible and interacted with. The agent should not just write a text file claiming the devices are configured or mock the UI.

integrity.description: Ensure the screenshot was genuinely captured from the screen.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent uses a screenshot tool (GUI or CLI) to capture the actual screen showing the Vital Signs app, rather than downloading a pre-made image or renaming an unrelated file.

integrity.description: Ensure the configuration report was genuinely created by the agent.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent writes the configuration report manually (via text editor or terminal commands) containing the specific devices and values, rather than just copying and pasting the exact task description prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring OpenICE with specific pediatric simulation values: Multiparameter Monitor (HR: 125 bpm, RR: 28 bpm, SpO2: 96%) and Infusion Pump (Rate: 10 mL/hr). The agent must verify data flow in the 'Vital Signs' application, save a screenshot to /home/ga/Desktop/pediatric_evidence.png, and write a configuration report to /home/ga/Desktop/pediatric_config.txt. All values are clinically reasonable for a pediatric profile.
